news.title:
IndiaMART InterMESH's Q2 results show EPS at ₹31.24, 56% above estimates, with shares up 4.6% to ₹2,211.
Analysts maintain a price target of ₹2,553, with revenue growth forecast at 16% by 2027, outpacing the industry.
news.summary:
IndiaMART InterMESH's Q2 results show EPS at ₹31.24, 56% above estimates, with shares up 4.6% to ₹2,211.
Analysts maintain a price target of ₹2,553, with revenue growth forecast at 16% by 2027, outpacing the industry.

news.title:
IndiaMART plans significant tech investments over two years, focusing on AI-driven tools to boost efficiency and supplier retention.
Q3 profit rose 56% YoY to ₹188 crore; shares surged 6% to ₹2,270 post Nalanda Fund's bulk deal worth ₹236 crore.
news.summary:
IndiaMART plans significant tech investments over two years, focusing on AI-driven tools to boost efficiency and supplier retention.
Q3 profit rose 56% YoY to ₹188 crore; shares surged 6% to ₹2,270 post Nalanda Fund's bulk deal worth ₹236 crore.

news.title:
IndiaMART surged 6.2%, hitting ₹2,277 intraday, on strong Q3 collections.
Stock traded 2.7% higher at ₹2,201 by 11:30 AM, beating Sensex's 0.8% drop.
news.summary:
IndiaMART surged 6.2%, hitting ₹2,277 intraday, on strong Q3 collections.
Stock traded 2.7% higher at ₹2,201 by 11:30 AM, beating Sensex's 0.8% drop.

Company plans slight digital marketing spend increase; unsure on INR 10 Cr budget timeline.

news.title:
Indiamart Intermesh expects stable paid supplier growth for another quarter, normalizing by April-June.
ARPU growth projected at 6-8% overall and 9-11% for top 10% customers, supported by trends.
news.summary:
Indiamart Intermesh expects stable paid supplier growth for another quarter, normalizing by April-June.

news.title:
IndiaMART's Q3 net profit rose 55.6% to ₹188.3 crore, revenue grew 13.4% to ₹401.6 crore.
EBITDA declined 2.9% to ₹134.5 crore, with margins dropping to 33.5% from 39.1% YoY.
news.summary:
IndiaMART's Q3 net profit rose 55.6% to ₹188.3 crore, revenue grew 13.4% to ₹401.6 crore.
EBITDA declined 2.9% to ₹134.5 crore, with margins dropping to 33.5% from 39.1% YoY.
